name: LG EXAONE Deep 32B
license: EXAONE AI Model License Agreement 1.1 - NC
archDisplay: Dense Transformer, 64 layers, GQA (40 Q-heads / 8 KV-heads), <thought>-tag CoT reasoning
note: Reasoning-specialized <thought>-tag CoT model rivaling much larger models on AIME/MATH.

results.notes: MMLU accuracy for the 32B model, confirmed via two independent fetches of the arXiv paper (html v1 and v2, Table 3). Not present on the abbreviated GitHub README / HF model-card comparison table, only in the full paper.
MMLU-Pro accuracy for the 32B model, confirmed via two independent fetches of the arXiv paper (html v1 and v2, Table 3).
AIME 2025, pass@1 (80.0 consistency@64). Distinct sitting from the existing AIME 2024 result above; both appear in Table 2 of the arXiv paper (2503.12524) and the GitHub README performance table.

verificationNotes: Existence and release date (2025-03-18) confirmed on GitHub README and HF model card. Params (30.95B, rounded 32B), 32K context, and 64-layer/GQA(40Q/8KV) architecture confirmed via HF card and independent search (model shares EXAONE-3.5-32B backbone). All 4 original benchmark rows (MATH-500 95.7, AIME2024 72.1/90.0, GPQA-Diamond 66.1, LiveCodeBench 59.5) verbatim-confirmed on both the HF card and GitHub README — no changes. 2026-07 completeness pass: read the full arXiv paper (2503.12524, both html v1/v2 and the extracted PDF, all 14 pages incl. appendices) plus the official LG AI Research EXAONE Deep Model Card PDF, the GitHub README raw markdown, and the HF model card. The paper's Table 2/3/4 and the README table are exhaustive for this model — the only evaluation categories LG published are: MATH-500, AIME 2024, AIME 2025, Korean CSAT Math 2025 (no taxonomy slug — not mapped), GPQA Diamond, LiveCodeBench, MMLU, and MMLU-Pro. AIME 2025 pass@1 (65.8, cons@64 80.0) was the one result present in the source tables but missing from the corpus; added above. No IFEval, no agentic/tool-use, no coding benchmark beyond LiveCodeBench, no additional MMLU-family variant, no IFEval/instruction-following, and no IFEval-adjacent long-context or safety table exist in any official EXAONE Deep source — the paper's own Limitations section confirms these models were not evaluated for general instruction-following (users are redirected to the EXAONE 3.5 Instruct line for that). Checked the newer K-EXAONE technical report (arXiv 2601.01739) for any retroactive comparison rows against EXAONE Deep 32B; it only benchmarks against EXAONE 4.0, not EXAONE Deep. No independent/third-party leaderboard (LiveCodeBench site, Chatbot Arena/LMArena, Open LLM Leaderboard, Artificial Analysis) publishes EXAONE Deep 32B scores beyond the vendor's own self-reported table, consistent with its research-only NC license limiting broad third-party hosting.
